Next frontier in human connection technology
Big tech is digitizing everything.
Except the thing that matters most in our lives — real human connection.
Scroll alone
→
need for real connection and community going parabolic
→
Feel good together
Built by hand
Our communities run on human connection.
That's what they are.
Finding your people is slow & hard.
Giving freely beyond our inner circles feels risky.
Communities break into cliques.
But no technology measures and powers that reality.
📱

Screen Isolation

Smartphones captured our attention. Social media promised connection but delivered loneliness.

🤖

AI Displacement

AI agents and robotics are displacing human labor, purpose, and identity.

🎭

Synthetic Social

Social media is becoming less real. AI-generated content is replacing authentic human expression.

THE VOID

The need for
real human connection,
community, and purpose
is about to go parabolic

Appendix · The Social Intelligence Layer
The Private Social Layer: what skill ratings can't see.
What DUPR Knows
3.5
One number.
Every matchmaking decision.
What Give n Receive Captures — The Private Social Layer
Social Compatibility & Personality
  • Who nobody wants to play with
  • Who everyone wants to play with — your most valuable members
  • Who brings incredible energy & generosity to every session
  • Who kills the vibe
  • Who is the social glue holding a group together
  • Who cliques up and excludes others
  • Who lifts weaker players vs makes them feel like a burden
  • Who celebrates great shots — even opponents' — vs only cares about winning
  • Who complains constantly vs stays positive when losing
  • Who has great court etiquette vs who doesn't
  • Who is humble vs arrogant about their level
  • Who trash-talks fun vs crosses the line
  • Who makes people laugh on court
  • Who tries to coach others unsolicited
Play Style Preferences
  • Who lobs every point — and who can't stand lobbers
  • Who bangs every ball — and who hates bangers
  • Who poaches every shot & never lets their partner play
  • Who sandbags below their real level
  • Who only wants competitive vs who only wants social
  • Who wants fast-paced vs prefers slower strategic
  • Who thrives in mixed doubles vs only same-gender
  • Who plays best at 7am vs comes alive at evening
  • Who wants to drill & improve vs just rally
  • Who stacks in doubles vs plays traditional sides
Reliability & Behavior
  • Who flakes last-minute every other week vs never cancels
  • Who argues line calls
  • Who shows up late and holds up the court
  • Who is coachable & improving fast vs plateaued & frustrated
  • Who brings great guests vs guests who wreck the vibe
  • Who refers every new member vs never brings anyone
Life Context
  • Who is brand new and needs to be welcomed carefully
  • Who is thinking about leaving and nobody knows yet
  • Who has a bad knee and needs partners who won't run them ragged
  • Who drives 45 minutes and needs the session to be worth it
  • Who plays best with whom — from real experience, not a number
  • Who just went through something tough and needs community now
  • Couples that play great together vs couples that fight on court
  • Who has quiet beef with whom
The Play-Up Problem
95% of players want to play up. DUPR punishes strong players for playing down. GnT is a value marketplace: give energy, reliability, referrals → earn your dream match.
Why Full Courts Are Vulnerable
Retention time-bomb: bad matches → quiet churn. A club next door running GnT social intelligence delivers dramatically better experiences. Seasonal churn: members cancel the moment outdoor courts open.
Operational Precision
Exact headcount management — precisely the right number every session. Intelligent substitution: cancellation filled with the right replacement who fits skill, style & social dynamics.
The Data Exists
The most valuable,
networkable, untapped
dataset on the planet
is trapped inside
all of our heads.
Give n Receive

The Cost of Honesty
You'd never
say it out loud.
“I don't want to play with Mike.” “He's not really a 4.0.” “That group makes me dread Wednesday.” “Sarah is the only reason I come on Tuesdays.” “I give all the energy and never get it back.” “I stopped coming because of one person.”

Different Layer of the Stack
Marketplaces light up cities.
Give ‘n Receive rewires communities that already exist.
Same cultural wave (movement as the new social currency). Different layer of the stack.
Dimension
The Marketplace
e.g. SweatPals, Eventbrite
Give ‘n Receive
The operating system
Who it’s for
Strangers looking for a class to attend in their city
Existing real-world communities running on human value exchange — coordination stacks stuck in 2005
Atomic unit
The event — broadcast → audience
The invitation — directional → small group
What it does
Discovery + ticketing + payments — host posts, anyone signs up
Encodes the human coordinator’s tacit knowledge — picks the right four for a court
Data captured
Tickets, attendance, repeat bookings — same data Eventbrite has had for 15 years
Every invite, accept, decline, sub, fill — directional behavioral signal between specific humans
The moat
Two-sided liquidity per city — cold-start, executed well
The Invitation Graph — compounds with every invite, can’t be scraped or bought
How it scales
Add cities · one at a time
Add clubs · CourtReserve = 1,600 already · each one already has a Krista
They are the marketplace.
We are the operating system that runs underneath.

Core Product · Decisions in Motion
Every invite = a choice. Every choice = signal.
Real decisions captured, in real time. Not surveying who likes who. Who chooses to spend time with who.

Will they be nice?
They're the best. You'll feel at home.

Pickleball is the lowest-risk wedge.

Fastest-growing sport in US history

4 players / court

Densest social signal per session

3–4x / week

Pickleball is the lowest-risk path to the world's first autonomous human connection network.
Pickleball is the most community-oriented sport ever invented.

sport in U.S. history.

Wildly social.
High velocity.

broken for social players
and stuck in 2005.

The Krista loop
Krista's judgment, captured.
Every time AI proposes a group and Krista corrects it, the correction becomes a training label. Two years of corrections. The training data competitors don't have.
AI proposed
Sarah + Tom + Jen + Dan · Tue 6pm
→
Krista corrected
Swap Tom for Bob
Tom's pace is too aggressive for Sarah's skill
Label: pace mismatch
AI proposed
Renee invited — Sat 9am pickup
→
Krista corrected
Hold — Renee just had a tough match
Send a softer Tuesday-evening invite first
Label: emotional re-entry timing
AI proposed
Group of 4 same-rating players
→
Krista corrected
Add Jess as anchor — group hasn't gelled
Three quiet players need a connector personality
Label: social anchor needed
AI proposed
Mike + Pat — played 4 weeks straight
→
Krista corrected
Break the pattern — cross-pollinate
Same group too long becomes a clique; introduce Linda
Label: anti-clique rotation
Every correction is a label. Every label is a moat. Two years of these. The data no one can buy.
Why now
Three forces just converged. None of them existed at scale a year ago.
Once-in-a-decade alignment. We sit at the intersection — and we've been running 2 years ahead.
Force 1 · Compute
AI just got cheap.
~95% drop in per-decision LLM cost in three years. AI economics that work for community-scale apps weren't possible in 2022. They are now.
Force 2 · Wedge
Pickleball just exploded.
Fastest-growing sport in US history. 19.8M players. 18.8M still building it by hand. The wedge market just appeared in front of us.
Force 3 · Demand
Loneliness hit crisis.
$500B/year cost. US Surgeon General declared a national health emergency. Algorithmic feeds make it worse. Real-world connection is the only fix.
Cheap intelligence + a fast-densifying social graph + an existential demand spike. This is the window. We've been running 2 years inside it.
Our Promise

Dave Velardo · dave@givenreceive.com · 978-604-0223
Why Now
Three forces converging.
None of them existed at this scale a year ago.
Once-in-a-decade alignment. We sit at the intersection.
Force 1 · Compute
AI just got cheap.
~95% drop in per-decision LLM cost in 3 years. Per-player, per-court AI economics now work for community-scale apps. Wasn't possible in 2022.
Force 2 · Wedge
Pickleball just exploded.
90+ new clubs/month. Fastest-growing sport in US history. 19.8M players, 18.8M coordinating by hand. The wedge market just appeared.
Force 3 · Demand
Loneliness reached crisis.
$500B/yr economic cost. US Surgeon General declared it a national health emergency. AI-driven feeds making it parabolic. Real-world connection is the only fix.
Cheap intelligence + a fast-densifying social graph + an existential demand spike. This is the window.
Why We Win
Every existing player only sees half the equation.
We're the only player on the connection axis. The graph is the moat.
Multi-player · Connection ↑
↓ Single-player · Rating only
Score-only ←
→ Coordination · Graph

We sit between. The first to write it down keeps it.
Distribution
How we get to 500 clubs.
Founder-led → engineered. The playbook is NEPC, repeated.
1
Year 1 · Founder-led
30 clubs
$30K design-partner ARR
  • NEPC referrals + Krista's club-owner network
  • Dave-led sales, hand-picked 50 clubs
  • White-glove onboarding (we set it up for them)
  • Goal: prove the playbook is repeatable
2
Year 2 · Inside sales
175 clubs
$1.27M ARR · Series A signal
  • Repeatable onboarding: signed → live in <30 days
  • 2-rep inside-sales team (founder still closes top 20%)
  • Channel partnerships (CourtReserve / DUPR co-sell)
  • Goal: $1M ARR for Series A defensibility
3
Year 3 · Self-serve + outbound
500 clubs
$5.26M ARR · category leader
  • PLG: club-owner waitlist + referral loop unlocked
  • 3-rep outbound team for enterprise multi-club operators
  • D2P launch: $20/mo player tier on top of B2B graph
  • Goal: 1% of US clubs & the category's standard graph
Unit Economics CAC < $1.2K · Payback <6 mo · LTV/CAC 10:1+ · Gross margin 85%

The Flywheel
Every signal makes the next one more valuable.
Players, clubs, signals, matches. Compounding network effects. The graph is the moat.
More players → denser graph → better matches → higher retention → more clubs sign on.
Each turn of the wheel makes the moat deeper.
What This Becomes
Each of these owns one slice. We sit at the intersection.
Comparable outcomes already exist — fragmented across categories. We unify them on one graph.
Solo Activity
Strava
$2B+ valuation
Solo runs/rides. Single-player. No group graph. Charges $12/mo.
Online Community
Discord
$15B valuation
Group chat for online tribes. No real-world coordination. No physical layer.
Gather IRL
Eventbrite
$3B+ valuation
One-off events. No recurring graph. Transactional, not relational.
B2B Fitness
Mindbody
$1.9B acquisition
Booking + CRM for studios. No connection layer. Operations-only.
Give n Receive
Strava's economics · Discord's community · Eventbrite's recurrence · Mindbody's distribution
All four — on one graph nobody else owns.
North Star · 10-year vision
We're building the map of human connection
— one invitation at a time.
10-Year North Star
In ten years…
Every social community in the world
runs on the Give n Receive graph.
Voice of the Community
What real members actually say when nobody's pitching them.
Six months of unsolicited feedback at NEPC. Receipts available on request.
“Finally, I feel like a person, not a rating.”
Member · Laurie Relinski
“I don't even check the calendar anymore. The invitations just show up — and they're always right.”
Member · Dean Taylor
“The GnR invite has three courts all month. The skill rating event couldn't fill one.”
Krista Trefethen · NEPC General Manager
“I drove 45 minutes for the first time in months. The group felt right before I got there.”
Member · Tom Flanigan
“I'm new and was nervous. The first invite said: 'three players who love welcoming new people.' That's the whole game.”
Member · Joe Army
“You don't realize how much mental load Krista was carrying until the system carries it. Then you can't go back.”
Member · Jen Roopenian
Every quote unsolicited. Every member retained. This is what product-market fit sounds like.

Why Now
Why now?
Enabling wedge-problem
The fastest growing real-world social network post-COVID is trapped in broken legacy infrastructure
Enabling technology
Only a new class of AI-Agents can rapidly learn human connection & human value
Enabling core product
Invitation-feed measures Give n Receive as it schedules it
Enabling data
Human uniqueness measured by the private thoughts and feelings we have about each other
Enabling incentive flywheel
The value you give is measured implicitly and comes back to you via the people and experiences you value most

Each gate unlocks the next. No skipping ahead.

We don't skip to autonomous matching. We earn it. Every gate trains the system on a harder version of the same problem.

Network of clubs

North Star
Abundant human connection becomes the default for all social sports communities.
The Problem
Ratings tell you who to compete against.
They don't tell you who to play with.
~5%
of pickleballers are rated
SFIA 2025 · DUPR public data
The other 95% — the people who actually keep clubs alive — the industry doesn't see.
📊Industry Assumption
Infrastructure for the 5%. Ratings, matchmaking, leagues — but what about the other 95%?
🔧Built By Hand
Finding your people is slow. Reaching beyond your circle feels risky. Communities become cliques.
🚫Dead End
Ratings aren't accurate — and the industry doubles down with more algorithms for the last 100 years' paradigm.
👁Invisible Reality · our wedge
Every player knows who brings great energy, who flakes, who's the social glue. That drives every decision — and no system captures it.
The Urgency
Pickleball desperately
needs this new paradigm.
Fastest-growing sport in U.S. history.
Still matched by skill rating alone.
The Next Frontier
Social media is a very narrow view
of human connection technology.
Scroll alone
→
AI → need for real connection & community going parabolic
→
Feel good together
Our vision
All our lives run on
Abundant
human connection
Every prior substrate of civilization — food, water, information, money — has gone abundant.
Human connection is next.

What The World Needs · Variant B
Every variable humans need has collapsed.
Evolved for
Modern life delivers
Connection
Tribes 50–150 → Apartments alone
Real contact

Belonging

Diagnosis
Every metric of human flourishing collapses.
What The World Needs · Variant C
Humans were never meant
to live for themselves.
Every wisdom tradition agrees. Every modern science agrees. Cooperation is the only operating system that ever worked — and it's the one we're optimizing away from.
Loneliness epidemic
Screen addiction
Depression surge
Chronic illness
Self-obsession
GnR rebuilds the conditions for the only life that ever worked.
Two Imbalances
Two imbalances rule modern life.
Values Imbalance
Deep down, we feel what we need — each other.
We just forgot to listen.
Tech Imbalance
A multi-trillion-dollar stack.
No human connection tech.
Two Imbalances · Variant B
Modern life weighs the wrong things.
GnR rebalances
Tech that measures connection. · Culture that rewards giving.
Tech Imbalance
Tech built for everything except connection.
A trillion-dollar stack pointed at attention, status, productivity, and transactions. Not at the thing the science says matters most.

Human Connection
#1 predictor of a fulfilling life
Every credible study agrees. No trillion-dollar company has been built for it.
Value Imbalance
We measure the wrong things. We reward the wrong things.
Modern life ranks money, fame, intelligence, and work. Fulfillment science ranks giving, connection, presence. The two lists barely touch.
What culture rewards
💰Money
🏆Fame
🧠Intelligence
💼Work
What fulfillment science says matters
🤲GivingMost undervalued
💞Connection
🌿Presence
✨Human spirit
Two lists. Almost no overlap. We're optimizing for the wrong rank.
Why GnR Exists
GnR corrects both.
A technology layer for connection. A culture layer for giving. The Invitation Graph runs both.
Tech layer
We measure human connection.
Every invitation, every group, every accept and decline. Captured. Compounded. Acted on. The first technology built for the thing tech forgot.
Human layer
We reward giving.
The most undervalued trait in modern life becomes the through-line of the product. Givers earn standing. Communities run on reciprocity. We make the right thing the rewarded thing.
The science of connection is settled. The infrastructure for it begins here.

The Paradigm for the Next 100 Years
A skill number
doesn't tell you…
Who everyone wants to play with — and who nobody wants on their court.
Who brings incredible energy vs who kills the vibe.
Who flakes last-minute vs who never cancels.
Who lifts weaker players up vs who makes them feel like a burden.
3.5 Legacy Approach
Reduces every player to a single number. DUPR punishes strong players for playing with weaker ones. Disincentivizes generosity.
🌐 Give n Receive Approach
Measures each person's uniqueness — energy, reliability, social contribution, play style — through private social signals.
